title: pulsatilla angustifolia subsp. flavescens
intro: Pulsatilla angustifolia subsp. flavescens, also known as pasque flower, is a plant species native to North America that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Pulsatilla angustifolia subsp. flavescens include its ability to aid in respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating menstrual irregularities and menopausal symptoms. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
